what_it_does: A self-running acceptance/unit test file for the introspection core. It uses stub platform entries and stub discoverers to verify that the leak gate, transport-invariant derivation, classification rules, CLI-help parsing, floor guard, missing-adapter handling, and engine classify/project/refresh paths behave as specified without spawning a live platform binary.
observations: Implements a tiny PASS/FAIL harness via the local `ok` helper instead of using an external test framework.
Starts with a grep gate over engine.py, rules.py, discover.py, and every adapter file for banned platform-name literals.
Tests `rules.derive_transport_invariants` with a neutral stub head builder and body-key overrides.
Includes an R6 swap-kind test that excludes head flags which also appear on capability axes from the R1 locked set.
Exercises the five classification rules R1 through R5 and the R4 novelty fail-closed path.
Parses a tiny help text with `CliHelpDiscoverer` and checks that a sub-floor parse raises.
Monkeypatches `introspection.discover.DISCOVERERS` to inject a stub discoverer for the floor-guard test.
Verifies `select_discoverer` raises `MissingAdapterError` naming the missing class for an unbuilt adapter.
Drives `engine.classify_platform`, `engine.project`, and `engine.refresh` through stub discover functions.
Checks refresh diff shape, added/changed/vanished/unclassified buckets, and an empty-diff-on-version-bump guard.
Ends with an empty-R1-set guard that asserts the engine raises rather than silently demoting locked flags.
standouts: The F-FIX-10 leak gate: a file-level grep that fails the suite if any Level-1 code contains platform-specific literals.
The R6 swap-kind head-default exclusion test, which shows head flags that are also capability-axis members become consent rather than locked.
The GENPROOF-converse test that an unbuilt discoverer fails loud with the missing class name.
The empty-diff-on-version-bump broken-read guard in the refresh path.
The entire suite runs without a live platform process, using stubs and monkeypatching.
conventions: Docstrings and section comments map every test block to a requirement ID (C-CORE-*, F-FIX-*, GENPROOF-converse).
Uses neutral stub tokens in the test itself so the test does not violate the same leak gate it checks.
Prefers fail-loud assertions: expects `RuntimeError`, `ValueError`, or `MissingAdapterError` rather than silent defaults.
Uses `model_copy` to create mutated platform/signal-set variants for negative cases.
Imports domain contracts (`CapabilityEntry`, `PlatformEntry`, etc.) from a `contracts` package.
concerns: `floor_discover` is defined but never used; it appears to be dead code.
Monkeypatching the global `DISCOVERERS` registry is fragile and relies on the `finally` block to restore state.
The test file mixes acceptance-level intent with low-level unit-test mechanics such as direct registry patching.
Hardcoded paths like `/bin/tool` and `/bin/tool` in stubs are not portable, though they are only strings for tests.
Some comments reference LEAD-only live checks that are not implemented or queued in this file.
notes: A stub-driven acceptance harness that proves the introspection core's rule engine and fail-loud guards without touching a real platform binary.
questions: What is the exact signature and behavior of `rules.derive_transport_invariants` in the production modules?
Where is the `CapabilityEntry` contract defined and what fields does it expose beyond those used here?
How does `engine.refresh` compute 'changed' entries when the prior and current sets differ in posture?
What is the LEAD queue mechanism for the live CliHelp/StreamInit items mentioned in the docstring?
What is the structure of `introspection.discover.DISCOVERERS` and how are real adapter classes registered?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to verify the introspection core by use with stubs while leaving live binary tests to the lead; the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a runnable, no-external-spawn acceptance proof that the introspection engine and rules enforce the intended security and correctness invariants.
novelty: It combines a static grep 'leak gate' over Level-1 source files with functional rule-engine tests in a single self-contained acceptance script, and it explicitly tests the R6 swap-kind head-default exclusion.
contribution: A runnable, stub-driven acceptance test that documents and verifies the expected contracts of the introspection gate, rule priorities, transport-invariant derivation, floor guard, missing-adapter error, and refresh diff guards.
summary_for_embedding: Self-contained acceptance test for the introspection core that verifies leak gate, rule classification, transport invariant derivation, CLI-help parsing, missing-adapter failure, and engine refresh guards using stub platforms and discoverers.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a no-live-spawn verification harness that proves the introspection engine and rules fail loud and classify capabilities correctly.

symbols.does: Records a pass/fail result and prints a concise test outcome.
symbols.description: The local assertion helper used throughout the file. It appends the test name to PASS or FAIL, prints PASS/FAIL, and optionally prints a detail string only when the assertion fails.

symbols.does: Returns a neutral stub command list for transport-invariant derivation tests.
symbols.description: A test helper that produces a generic command-line head containing unconditional flags. It uses neutral tokens so the test itself does not introduce platform-specific literals while exercising `rules.derive_transport_invariants`.

symbols.does: Stub discoverer class that feeds fixed capability rows to the engine.
symbols.description: Implements the discoverer interface expected by the engine with a fixed `source_type` and a `parse` method that returns pre-canned entries, enabling deterministic classify/project/refresh tests without a live spawn.

symbols.does: Stores the fixed list of capability entries.
symbols.description: Initializes the stub discoverer with a list of `CapabilityEntry` objects that will be returned by `parse`.
The discover method returns the string 'raw' because the actual data is supplied by the stub's `parse` method.

symbols.does: Returns the stored entries regardless of input.
symbols.description: Ignores its arguments and returns the entries provided at construction time, making engine tests deterministic and independent of real CLI output.
Factory that creates a `PlatformEntry` with a stub executable locator, a cli-help discovery source, a configurable floor guard, signal sets, and body-key overrides used across multiple test blocks.

symbols.does: Unused stub function returning parsed rows.
symbols.description: Defined to mimic a discover function that returns the tiny parsed help rows, but it is not referenced anywhere in the active test flow.

symbols.does: Returns the base fixture capability rows.
symbols.description: A stub discover function that returns the five fixture entries used to test classification, projection, and refresh behavior.

symbols.does: Returns fixture rows plus a new flag for refresh diff tests.
symbols.description: A stub discover function that adds `--brand-new` to the base fixture so the refresh path can surface an added entry and exercise the R4 unmatched fail-closed posture.

symbols.does: Returns unchanged prior rows to test empty-diff guards.
symbols.description: A stub discover function that returns exactly the prior entries, used to verify that a version bump with no diff raises while a same-version empty diff is allowed.
